subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Risk Assessment and Risk Appetite Statement
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for conducting comprehensive risk assessments to identify and evaluate the inherent risks associated with customers, products, and services. It also establishes guidelines for defining the institution's risk appetite statement, which articulates the level of risk the institution is willing to accept in pursuit of its strategic objectives.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct comprehensive risk assessments to identify and evaluate ML/TF risks. WHO: Risk management team and compliance officers. WHEN: Annually or upon significant changes in customer profiles or regulatory requirements. HOW: Utilize risk assessment frameworks and tools to analyze customer data, transaction patterns, and external risk factors. WHERE: Applicable to all customer segments and geographic locations where the institution operates.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for collecting and verifying additional information on high-risk customers, including their source of funds and source of wealth. It emphasizes the need for enhanced monitoring of customer transactions and ongoing updates to customer profiles.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Collect and verify additional customer information, including occupation, source of funds, and source of wealth. WHO: Customer onboarding team and compliance officers. WHEN: At the time of onboarding and periodically thereafter, at least annually. HOW: Implement enhanced due diligence procedures, including public database checks and media searches. WHERE: Applicable to all high-risk customers across all business lines.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirement for obtaining senior management approval before establishing or continuing business relationships with high-risk customers. It ensures that all high-risk engagements are reviewed and sanctioned at the executive level.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Obtain written approval from senior management for high-risk customer relationships. WHO: Relationship managers and senior management. WHEN: Prior to onboarding high-risk customers or when significant changes occur in the customer's risk profile. HOW: Document the approval process and maintain records for audit purposes. WHERE: Applicable to all high-risk customer engagements within the institution.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for verifying the source of wealth for clients during the onboarding process. It specifies the steps to be taken to gather, assess, and document information related to the client's wealth.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Collect detailed information on the client's total net worth and source of wealth. WHO: Compliance officers and onboarding staff are responsible for gathering this information. WHEN: This information must be collected during the initial onboarding phase and updated annually or when significant changes occur. HOW: Use a combination of client interviews, public records, and third-party verification sources to gather information. WHERE: This process applies to all client onboarding activities across all branches and online platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for ongoing monitoring of client information and periodic refreshing of data to ensure continued compliance with EDD standards.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a system for ongoing monitoring of client transactions and periodic updates of client information. WHO: Compliance and risk management teams are responsible for monitoring and updating client data. WHEN: Monitoring should occur continuously, with formal reviews conducted at least annually. HOW: Utilize automated systems to flag unusual transactions and schedule regular reviews of client information. WHERE: This applies to all client accounts and transactions processed through the institution's systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for identifying and verifying the identity of clients in private banking and wealth management, particularly focusing on high-risk individuals and PEPs.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct thorough identification and verification of clients using reliable and independent sources of information. WHO: Compliance officers and relationship managers are responsible for executing this process. WHEN: This process must be completed prior to the establishment of any client relationship and updated annually or upon significant changes in client circumstances. HOW: Utilize government-issued identification, financial statements, and third-party verification services to confirm client identity. WHERE: This control applies to all client onboarding processes across all branches and digital platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the assessment of the source of wealth for all high-net-worth clients, ensuring that their financial background is legitimate and transparent.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Assess and document the source of wealth for all clients, particularly those classified as high-risk or PEPs. WHO: Relationship managers and compliance teams are responsible for conducting these assessments. WHEN: Assessments must be completed during the onboarding process and reviewed annually or when there are significant changes in client circumstances. HOW: Collect and analyze documentation such as tax returns, business ownership records, and investment portfolios. WHERE: This assessment applies to all private banking clients across all operational jurisdictions.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for ongoing monitoring of client transactions to identify and mitigate potential risks associated with money laundering and financial crimes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement continuous monitoring of client transactions, focusing on high-value and high-frequency transactions. WHO: Compliance teams are responsible for monitoring and reporting suspicious activities. WHEN: Monitoring must occur in real-time, with periodic reviews conducted monthly. HOW: Utilize automated transaction monitoring systems and manual reviews to identify red flags. WHERE: This control applies to all transaction processing systems used in private banking and wealth management.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Training and Awareness for Front-Line Staff
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the training and continuous education of front-line staff in private banking to ensure they are equipped to perform Enhanced Due Diligence effectively. Training programs must cover risk identification, EDD procedures, and regulatory compliance.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct mandatory EDD training sessions for all front-line staff in private banking. WHO: Training will be the responsibility of the Compliance Department, with input from subject matter experts. WHEN: Training sessions must be conducted annually, with additional sessions scheduled as needed based on regulatory updates or identified risks. HOW: Training will utilize a combination of in-person workshops, online courses, and assessments to ensure comprehension. WHERE: Training will be conducted at the main office and via online platforms for remote staff.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Risk Assessment Procedures for High-Value Transactions
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for conducting risk assessments on high-value transactions, including those involving precious metals, stones, and art. It requires detailed documentation and analysis of each transaction's risk factors.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a standardized risk assessment framework for evaluating high-value transactions. WHO: The Risk Management Team will be responsible for conducting assessments, with oversight from the Compliance Department. WHEN: Risk assessments must be performed prior to the approval of any high-value transaction and reviewed quarterly. HOW: Assessments will utilize a risk scoring system based on predefined criteria, including transaction type, client profile, and geographic risk. WHERE: Assessments will be documented in the transaction management system and reviewed during compliance audits.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Oversight and Audit of EDD Practices
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the framework for regular oversight and auditing of EDD practices within private banking to ensure compliance and effectiveness in mitigating financial crime risks.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct regular audits of EDD practices and compliance with this policy. WHO: The Internal Audit Team will perform audits, with findings reported to senior management and the Board. WHEN: Audits will be conducted semi-annually, with additional audits triggered by significant changes in risk profiles or regulatory requirements. HOW: Audits will include reviews of transaction records, staff training logs, and risk assessment documentation. WHERE: Audits will cover all branches and departments involved in private banking operations.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Risk Assessment Procedures for High-Risk Customers
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for conducting risk assessments on high-risk customers, including the criteria for identifying such customers and the methodology for evaluating their risk profiles.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct comprehensive risk assessments for all high-risk customers, including the evaluation of customer profiles, transaction histories, and geographic risk factors. WHO: Compliance officers and risk management teams are responsible for conducting these assessments. WHEN: Risk assessments must be performed at the time of customer onboarding and reviewed annually or when significant changes occur in customer circumstances. HOW: Utilize risk assessment tools and methodologies to evaluate customer risk, including scoring systems and qualitative assessments. WHERE: Applicable to all customer accounts classified as high-risk within the institution's customer relationship management systems.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Training and Awareness for EDD Staff
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the training requirements for staff involved in Enhanced Due Diligence processes to ensure they are equipped to identify and manage risks associated with high-risk customers.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Provide training sessions on EDD processes, red flag identification, and regulatory requirements for all staff involved in customer due diligence. WHO: The training program will be developed and delivered by the compliance department, with participation from all relevant staff members. WHEN: Training must be conducted at least annually, with additional sessions provided as needed when regulations change or new risks are identified. HOW: Utilize a combination of in-person workshops, online training modules, and assessments to ensure understanding and retention of EDD principles. WHERE: Training will be conducted in the institution's training facilities and through online platforms accessible to all staff.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Audit and Monitoring of EDD Processes
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for auditing and monitoring the effectiveness of Enhanced Due Diligence processes to ensure compliance and identify areas for improvement.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct regular audits of EDD processes, including reviews of customer risk profiles and compliance with EDD procedures. WHO: Internal audit teams and compliance officers are responsible for conducting these audits. WHEN: Audits must be performed at least semi-annually, with additional audits triggered by significant findings or changes in risk profiles. HOW: Utilize audit checklists and sampling methods to evaluate compliance and effectiveness of EDD measures. WHERE: Audits will cover all departments involved in EDD processes and will review documentation stored in the institution's compliance management systems.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Risk Assessment and Risk Appetite Statement
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for conducting comprehensive risk assessments to identify and evaluate the inherent and residual risks associated with customers, products, and services. It also establishes guidelines for defining the institution's risk appetite statement, which articulates the level of risk the institution is willing to accept in pursuit of its strategic objectives.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct comprehensive risk assessments to identify ML/TF risks and define the risk appetite statement. WHO: Risk management team and compliance officers. WHEN: Annually or when significant changes occur in customer profiles or regulatory requirements. HOW: Utilize risk assessment frameworks and tools to evaluate risks and document findings. WHERE: Across all business units and customer segments within the institution.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Customer Due Diligence and Enhanced Monitoring
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for collecting and verifying additional customer information, including the source of funds and source of wealth. It also outlines the procedures for enhanced monitoring of transactions and customer behavior to detect suspicious activities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Collect and verify additional customer information and conduct enhanced monitoring of transactions. WHO: Customer onboarding teams and compliance analysts. WHEN: At the time of onboarding and continuously throughout the customer relationship. HOW: Implement automated monitoring systems and manual reviews of high-risk transactions. WHERE: In all customer accounts classified as high-risk.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Client Onboarding and Wealth Assessment Procedures
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the procedures for collecting and assessing information regarding a client's total net worth and source of wealth during the onboarding process. It emphasizes the importance of obtaining detailed and verifiable information from clients.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Financial institutions must collect detailed information on clients' total net worth and source of wealth during onboarding. WHO: Compliance officers and onboarding staff are responsible for gathering this information. WHEN: This information must be collected at the time of client onboarding. HOW: Use structured interviews and document requests to gather information, ensuring that responses are recorded and verified. WHERE: This process applies to all client onboarding activities across all branches and online platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the steps for verifying the source of wealth, including the collection of supporting documentation and the use of independent sources for validation.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Institutions must verify the source of wealth through a combination of client-provided information and independent documentation. WHO: Compliance teams and risk analysts are responsible for conducting the verification. WHEN: Verification must occur within 30 days of client onboarding or when significant transactions are identified. HOW: Utilize a risk-sensitive approach to assess the legitimacy of the wealth, including reviewing documents such as tax returns, bank statements, and third-party confirmations. WHERE: This verification process applies to all clients, with heightened scrutiny for PEPs and high-risk individuals.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for assessing the risk level of customers based on various factors, including geographic location, transaction history, and customer profile. It mandates the use of a standardized risk assessment tool to ensure consistency and accuracy.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a risk assessment for each customer using a standardized tool. WHO: Compliance officers are responsible for performing the assessments. WHEN: Assessments must be completed prior to onboarding and reviewed annually. HOW: Utilize the risk assessment tool to evaluate customer risk factors and document findings. WHERE: Applicable to all customer accounts across all branches and departments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for monitoring customer transactions for suspicious activity and reporting such activities to the appropriate authorities. It includes guidelines for setting thresholds for alerts and the process for escalating concerns.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Monitor all transactions for suspicious activity and report findings. WHO: Transaction monitoring team is responsible for daily monitoring and reporting. WHEN: Continuous monitoring with reports generated weekly. HOW: Use automated monitoring systems to flag transactions that exceed predefined thresholds. WHERE: Applies to all transaction processing systems within the institution.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates regular training for all employees on EDD procedures, recognizing suspicious activity, and understanding regulatory requirements. It aims to ensure that all staff are equipped to identify and respond to potential risks effectively.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Provide training sessions on EDD and AML compliance. WHO: All employees must attend training sessions conducted by the compliance department. WHEN: Training sessions will be held quarterly and upon onboarding of new staff. HOW: Utilize a combination of in-person workshops and online training modules. WHERE: Training will be conducted at all branch locations and through the institution's online learning platform.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for identifying and verifying the identity of customers engaged in international banking activities. It includes procedures for assessing the legitimacy of customer documentation and understanding the customer's economic profile.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Financial institutions must implement a robust customer identification and verification process. WHO: The compliance department is responsible for overseeing the implementation of this process. WHEN: This process must be conducted at the onboarding stage and reviewed annually or upon any significant change in customer circumstances. HOW: Institutions should utilize reliable identification methods, including government-issued IDs, and verify the authenticity of documents through independent sources. WHERE: This control applies to all customer onboarding processes across all branches and digital platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for the ongoing monitoring of customers identified as high-risk due to their international banking activities. It includes transaction monitoring and periodic reviews of customer profiles.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Financial institutions must conduct ongoing monitoring of high-risk customers, including transaction analysis and profile reviews. WHO: The risk management team is responsible for executing this monitoring process. WHEN: Monitoring should occur continuously, with formal reviews conducted quarterly. HOW: Institutions should employ automated transaction monitoring systems to flag suspicious activities and conduct manual reviews of flagged transactions. WHERE: This control applies to all customer accounts classified as high-risk within the institution's transaction processing systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for assessing and understanding complex ownership structures of customers engaged in international banking. It emphasizes the need for clarity regarding the beneficial ownership and legitimacy of legal arrangements.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Financial institutions must conduct thorough assessments of customers with complex ownership structures, including identifying all beneficial owners and understanding the purpose of the legal arrangements. WHO: The compliance officer is responsible for ensuring these assessments are performed. WHEN: Assessments should be conducted at onboarding and reviewed whenever there are changes in ownership or structure. HOW: Institutions should require detailed documentation of ownership structures and perform due diligence on all parties involved. WHERE: This control applies to all customers with complex structures across all operational environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for conducting a thorough review of the financial institution's risk assessment related to Enhanced Due Diligence. It specifies the need for regular updates and evaluations to ensure the risk assessment accurately reflects the institution's exposure to various risks.
subpolicies.Control: The compliance team (WHO) must conduct a comprehensive review of the risk assessment (WHAT) at least annually (WHEN) using established risk assessment frameworks and methodologies (HOW). This review should include an analysis of customer profiles, products, services, and geographic risks (WHERE) to ensure alignment with the institution's risk appetite and regulatory requirements.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the evaluation of existing policies and procedures related to Enhanced Due Diligence to ensure they are effective and aligned with the risk assessment.
subpolicies.Control: The internal audit team (WHO) will evaluate the policies and procedures (WHAT) bi-annually (WHEN) by reviewing documentation, conducting interviews with staff, and assessing compliance with established guidelines (HOW). The evaluation will focus on the effectiveness of customer onboarding processes and transaction monitoring (WHERE).

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for training staff involved in Enhanced Due Diligence processes to ensure they possess the necessary skills and knowledge.
subpolicies.Control: The human resources department (WHO) must implement a comprehensive training program (WHAT) for all staff involved in EDD, including new hires and existing employees (WHEN). Training sessions should be conducted quarterly (HOW) and cover topics such as risk assessment, customer profiling, and regulatory compliance (WHERE). Attendance records and knowledge assessments must be maintained to ensure competency.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes requirements for documentation and record-keeping related to Enhanced Due Diligence processes to ensure compliance and facilitate audits.
subpolicies.Control: All departments involved in EDD (WHO) must maintain accurate and comprehensive records of customer profiles, risk assessments, and audit findings (WHAT) for a minimum of five years (WHEN). Documentation must be organized and easily accessible (HOW) to support internal and external audits (WHERE).

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the procedures for monitoring transactions and investigating suspicious activities as part of the EDD process. It outlines the criteria for identifying high-risk customers and the steps to be taken when suspicious transactions are detected.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a transaction monitoring system that flags suspicious activities based on predefined criteria. WHO: Compliance officers are responsible for monitoring transactions and initiating investigations. WHEN: Monitoring should occur in real-time, with daily reviews of flagged transactions. HOW: Utilize automated tools for transaction monitoring and manual reviews for flagged transactions. WHERE: This control applies to all transaction processing systems used by the institution.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Audit Follow-Up and Remediation Action Plans
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for developing and implementing remediation action plans following EDD audits. It specifies the responsibilities of management and the audit team in ensuring that recommendations are acted upon.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop a remediation action plan that addresses all audit findings with clear priorities. WHO: Management of the relevant business lines is responsible for implementing the action plan. WHEN: Action plans must be developed within two weeks of the audit report and implemented within agreed timelines. HOW: Regular progress reports must be submitted to the audit committee. WHERE: This control applies to all business lines involved in EDD processes.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for conducting risk assessments on customers identified as high-risk. It includes guidelines for evaluating customer profiles, transaction patterns, and potential red flags.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a comprehensive risk assessment for each high-risk customer, evaluating their profile, transaction history, and any potential red flags. WHO: The compliance department is responsible for conducting the assessments, with input from customer service and risk management teams. WHEN: Risk assessments must be conducted at the time of onboarding and reviewed annually or when significant changes occur in the customer's profile. HOW: Utilize risk assessment tools and methodologies to evaluate customer risk levels, documenting findings in the customer file. WHERE: This control applies to all customer accounts classified as high-risk within the institution's CRM system.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for ongoing monitoring of high-risk customers and the reporting of suspicious activities. It emphasizes the importance of timely reporting to relevant authorities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement ongoing monitoring of transactions for high-risk customers to identify suspicious activities. WHO: The transaction monitoring team is responsible for monitoring and reporting, with oversight from the compliance department. WHEN: Monitoring must occur continuously, with suspicious activities reported immediately upon detection. HOW: Use automated transaction monitoring systems to flag unusual patterns and generate alerts for further investigation. WHERE: This control applies to all transactions processed through the institution's transaction monitoring systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for conducting comprehensive risk assessments to identify and evaluate ML/TF risks. It includes guidelines for data collection, risk factor analysis, and documentation of findings.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct comprehensive risk assessments that identify and evaluate ML/TF risks. WHO: Risk Management Department and Compliance Officers. WHEN: Annually, or more frequently if significant changes occur. HOW: Utilize established risk assessment frameworks and tools to analyze customer profiles, product offerings, and geographic exposure. WHERE: Across all operational units and jurisdictions where the institution operates.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the process for developing and maintaining the institution's risk appetite statement, including stakeholder engagement and approval processes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and maintain a risk appetite statement that defines acceptable risk levels. WHO: Senior Management and Board of Directors. WHEN: Reviewed and updated annually or as needed. HOW: Engage stakeholders in discussions to align risk appetite with strategic objectives and document the statement for approval. WHERE: At the institutional level, ensuring alignment across all departments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for obtaining and assessing information regarding a client's total net worth during the onboarding process. It emphasizes the importance of collecting comprehensive data to establish a baseline for further verification.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Collect general information on the client's total net worth during the onboarding process. WHO: Client onboarding team and compliance officers. WHEN: At the initial client onboarding stage. HOW: Utilize structured questionnaires and informal interviews to gather data. WHERE: In-person meetings, virtual consultations, or through secure online forms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy provides guidelines for obtaining information on the source of wealth from clients and other reliable sources. It includes methods for collecting data through interviews and public records.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Obtain detailed information on the source of wealth through client interviews and public sources. WHO: Compliance officers and client relationship managers. WHEN: During the client onboarding and ongoing monitoring phases. HOW: Conduct informal interviews and research public records, including media outlets and financial statements. WHERE: Client meetings, online databases, and public registries.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the verification process for the source of wealth, including the types of documentation required and the methods for assessing their validity.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Verify the source of wealth using a combination of client-provided documents and independent third-party sources. WHO: Compliance officers and risk assessment teams. WHEN: Upon receipt of client information and periodically thereafter based on risk assessment. HOW: Review and validate documents such as tax returns, bank statements, and third-party confirmations. WHERE: Within the compliance management system and relevant client files.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for collecting and verifying client information related to the source of funds and source of wealth. It mandates the collection of specific documentation and the verification of information through independent sources.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Collect detailed information about the source of funds and source of wealth from clients, including specific documentation such as bank statements, tax returns, and employment contracts. WHO: Compliance officers and client onboarding teams are responsible for collecting and verifying this information. WHEN: Information must be collected at the time of client onboarding and verified within 30 days. HOW: Use standardized forms and checklists to ensure all required information is gathered and verified. WHERE: This process applies to all client onboarding activities across all branches and online platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for the ongoing monitoring of transactions involving Politically Exposed Persons (PEPs) to ensure that their transactions are legitimate and consistent with their known sources of wealth.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement transaction monitoring systems to flag and review transactions involving PEPs for unusual patterns or amounts. WHO: Compliance monitoring teams are responsible for conducting these reviews. WHEN: Transactions must be monitored in real-time, with reviews conducted weekly for flagged transactions. HOW: Utilize automated monitoring tools that integrate with transaction processing systems to identify and alert on suspicious activities. WHERE: This monitoring applies to all transactions processed through the institution's systems, including online and branch transactions.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for collecting and verifying additional customer information necessary for Enhanced Due Diligence. It emphasizes the importance of understanding the customer's background, source of wealth, and ongoing monitoring of their transactional behavior.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Financial institutions must collect and verify additional information on customers, including occupation, volume of assets, and data from public databases. WHO: Compliance officers and customer service representatives are responsible for implementing this subpolicy. WHEN: Information must be collected at the time of onboarding and updated at least annually or when significant changes occur. HOW: Institutions will utilize automated systems for data collection and verification, supplemented by manual checks as necessary. WHERE: This control applies to all customer onboarding processes across all branches and digital platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for ongoing monitoring of customer transactions and the adjustment of customer profiles based on new information and behavioral changes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Institutions must conduct regular reviews of customer transactions and adjust profiles based on findings. WHO: Risk management teams and compliance officers are responsible for monitoring and adjustments. WHEN: Monitoring should occur continuously, with formal reviews conducted quarterly. HOW: Institutions will employ transaction monitoring systems and analytics to identify unusual patterns and behaviors. WHERE: This control is applicable to all customer accounts and transactions processed through the institution's systems.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for collecting and verifying customer information during the account opening process and throughout the customer relationship. It mandates the use of reliable sources for verification and establishes procedures for documenting the verification process.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Financial institutions must collect and verify customer identification information using reliable sources such as government-issued IDs and third-party verification services. WHO: The Compliance Department is responsible for overseeing the collection and verification process. WHEN: Customer information must be collected at account opening and verified within 5 business days. HOW: Institutions will utilize automated verification tools and manual checks to ensure accuracy. WHERE: This control applies to all customer onboarding processes across all branches and digital platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for ongoing monitoring of customer transactions to identify suspicious activities and ensure compliance with EDD standards. It includes guidelines for transaction analysis and escalation procedures.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Institutions must implement transaction monitoring systems that analyze customer transactions for unusual patterns or activities. WHO: The Risk Management Team is responsible for monitoring transactions and escalating suspicious activities to the Compliance Department. WHEN: Monitoring must occur in real-time, with daily reviews of flagged transactions. HOW: Institutions will use automated monitoring systems with predefined thresholds for alerts. WHERE: This control applies to all transaction processing systems used by the institution.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for periodically refreshing customer information to ensure its accuracy and relevance. It outlines the frequency and methods for conducting information updates.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Financial institutions must refresh customer information at least annually or upon significant changes in customer circumstances. WHO: The Customer Service Team is responsible for initiating the refresh process. WHEN: Information refreshes must be completed within 30 days of the scheduled review date. HOW: Institutions will utilize customer outreach methods, including emails and phone calls, to collect updated information. WHERE: This control applies to all customer accounts across all operational environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for identifying PEPs and conducting risk assessments to evaluate their potential risk levels. It includes guidelines for gathering information from reliable sources and documenting the assessment process.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a thorough identification process for all customers to determine PEP status using reliable databases and media sources. WHO: Compliance officers and customer onboarding teams are responsible for this process. WHEN: This identification must occur at the time of customer onboarding and be reviewed annually or upon any significant change in customer circumstances. HOW: Utilize automated screening tools and manual checks against PEP lists and media reports. WHERE: This control applies to all customer onboarding processes across all branches and online platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for ongoing monitoring of transactions involving PEPs to detect any suspicious activities or changes in risk profile.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement continuous monitoring of all transactions involving PEPs, including screening against updated PEP and sanctions lists. WHO: Transaction monitoring teams and compliance officers are responsible for this ongoing oversight. WHEN: Monitoring must occur in real-time for all transactions and be reviewed on a monthly basis for any flagged activities. HOW: Use transaction monitoring software to identify unusual patterns and generate alerts for further investigation. WHERE: This control is applicable across all transaction processing systems and platforms used by the institution.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the implementation of a structured process for conducting suitability and appropriateness assessments for all prospective clients seeking investment services. It requires financial institutions to gather detailed financial information and assess the client's investment knowledge, experience, and risk tolerance.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct suitability and appropriateness assessments for all prospective clients. WHO: Compliance officers and relationship managers are responsible for executing assessments. WHEN: Assessments must be completed prior to the approval of any investment service. HOW: Utilize standardized assessment tools and questionnaires to gather client information and evaluate their financial situation. WHERE: Assessments are to be conducted within the institution's CRM system and documented in client files.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Training and Specialization for Front-line Staff
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates specialized training for front-line staff in private banking to enhance their ability to perform Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) and identify potential financial crime risks. Training programs should cover the identification of red flags, risk assessment techniques, and compliance with regulatory requirements.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a comprehensive training program focused on EDD and financial crime risk identification. WHO: Training will be conducted by the Compliance Department and external experts. WHEN: Training sessions will be held quarterly, with mandatory refreshers every six months. HOW: Training effectiveness will be measured through assessments and feedback surveys. WHERE: Training will take place in designated training facilities and online platforms accessible to all relevant staff.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for assessing the risk of high-value and cross-border transactions in private banking. It requires that all transactions be evaluated against established risk criteria and documented accordingly.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct risk assessments for all high-value and cross-border transactions prior to approval. WHO: Transaction approval will be the responsibility of the Risk Management Team. WHEN: Assessments must be completed within 48 hours of transaction request. HOW: Risk assessments will utilize a standardized checklist and scoring system to evaluate transaction risks. WHERE: Assessments will be documented in the transaction management system.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Oversight and Monitoring of High-Value Assets
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes oversight mechanisms for managing high-value assets, including precious metals, stones, and art, to mitigate risks associated with their valuation and potential misuse.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement regular audits and monitoring of high-value asset transactions and valuations. WHO: The Internal Audit Department will conduct audits, with oversight from the Compliance Officer. WHEN: Audits will occur bi-annually, with additional reviews triggered by significant transactions. HOW: Audit findings will be documented and reported to senior management for review. WHERE: Audits will cover all locations where high-value assets are managed.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for verifying customer documentation to ensure authenticity and legitimacy. It includes guidelines for assessing the source of wealth and funds, as well as the need for additional documentation for high-risk customers.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Financial institutions must implement a verification process for all customer documentation, including source of wealth and funds. WHO: Compliance officers and customer service representatives are responsible for conducting verifications. WHEN: Documentation must be verified during the onboarding process and periodically thereafter, at least annually. HOW: Verification methods include cross-referencing documents with reliable sources, utilizing third-party verification services, and conducting interviews with customers. WHERE: This control applies to all customer onboarding processes across all branches and digital platforms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for ongoing monitoring of transactions for high-risk customers. It includes criteria for identifying suspicious activities and the procedures for reporting such activities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Financial institutions must implement ongoing monitoring of transactions for high-risk customers, focusing on identifying unusual patterns or suspicious activities. WHO: Transaction monitoring teams and compliance officers are responsible for monitoring and reporting. WHEN: Monitoring must occur in real-time for all transactions and reviewed at least monthly for high-risk accounts. HOW: Monitoring will utilize automated systems to flag unusual transactions, supplemented by manual reviews as necessary. WHERE: This control applies to all transaction processing systems and customer accounts identified as high-risk.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Identification and Verification of Beneficial Owners
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the identification and verification of all beneficial owners, directors, and relevant officers associated with complex legal structures. Financial institutions must ensure that all parties are documented and their identities verified to prevent misuse of the structures.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct thorough identification and verification of all beneficial owners and relevant officers. WHO: Compliance officers and risk management teams are responsible for this process. WHEN: This verification must occur during the customer onboarding process and be updated annually or upon any significant change. HOW: Utilize reliable third-party verification services and maintain records of all verification activities. WHERE: This control applies to all customer accounts involving complex legal structures across all operational environments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy requires financial institutions to assess the legitimacy and necessity of the legal arrangements used by customers with complex structures. Institutions must document the rationale behind the setup and ensure that it aligns with the customer's business activities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Evaluate the legal arrangements for legitimacy and necessity. WHO: Compliance analysts and legal advisors are responsible for conducting these assessments. WHEN: Assessments should be performed at the time of onboarding and reviewed annually or when there are changes in the structure. HOW: Review legal documentation such as trust deeds and foundation bylaws, and consult with legal experts as needed. WHERE: This assessment applies to all complex structures identified in customer accounts across all jurisdictions.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for monitoring transactions associated with complex legal structures to identify any unusual or suspicious activity that may indicate financial crime.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement transaction monitoring systems to detect unusual patterns. WHO: The compliance monitoring team is responsible for this activity. WHEN: Continuous monitoring must occur for all transactions, with periodic reviews conducted monthly. HOW: Utilize automated transaction monitoring tools and conduct manual reviews of flagged transactions. WHERE: This monitoring applies to all transactions processed through accounts with complex legal structures.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for reviewing the risk assessment documentation as part of the EDD audit process. Auditors must ensure that the risk assessment captures all relevant risks associated with customers, products, services, and geographies.
subpolicies.Control: Auditors (WHO) will review the risk assessment documentation (WHAT) at the beginning of the audit process (WHEN) to ensure it is comprehensive and up-to-date. This review will include verifying that the risk assessment reflects changes in the organization and regulatory environment (HOW) by comparing it against current market conditions and regulatory updates (WHERE).

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the evaluation of the financial institution's policies and procedures related to EDD. Auditors must assess whether these documents are aligned with the risk assessment and effectively mitigate identified risks.
subpolicies.Control: Auditors (WHO) will evaluate the policies and procedures (WHAT) during the audit (WHEN) to ensure they are risk-based and effective. This evaluation will involve checking for alignment with the risk assessment and the ability to develop customer-specific profiles (HOW) by reviewing documentation and interviewing relevant staff (WHERE).

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for assessing the training provided to staff involved in EDD processes. Auditors must ensure that staff have the necessary knowledge and skills to perform their duties effectively.
subpolicies.Control: Auditors (WHO) will review training records and materials (WHAT) during the audit (WHEN) to assess the adequacy of training provided to staff. This will include evaluating attendance records and knowledge testing results (HOW) to ensure comprehensive training is delivered (WHERE).

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for reviewing record-keeping practices related to EDD. Auditors must ensure that documentation is accurate, logical, and maintained for the required duration.
subpolicies.Control: Auditors (WHO) will examine the record-keeping practices (WHAT) during the audit (WHEN) to ensure compliance with local regulations and organizational policies. This will involve checking the accessibility and accuracy of records (HOW) to confirm that they are maintained in a secure and organized manner (WHERE).

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the process for following up on audit recommendations to ensure timely implementation and effectiveness of corrective actions.
subpolicies.Control: Management of relevant business lines (WHO) will track the implementation of audit recommendations (WHAT) following the audit report (WHEN). This will involve regular updates and reporting on progress (HOW) to ensure that all actions are completed in a timely manner (WHERE).

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the steps for planning and preparing for EDD audits, including defining the audit scope, objectives, and methodology tailored to the institution's specific needs.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop an audit plan that outlines the scope, objectives, and methodology for the EDD audit. WHO: The compliance officer and internal audit team are responsible for creating the audit plan. WHEN: The audit plan must be developed at least 30 days prior to the audit start date. HOW: Utilize risk assessment tools and previous audit findings to inform the planning process. WHERE: The audit plan should be applicable to all relevant departments and systems involved in EDD.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the procedures for executing the EDD audit, including data collection, analysis, and evaluation of EDD processes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Execute the audit plan by collecting and analyzing data related to EDD processes. WHO: The internal audit team, led by a qualified auditor, is responsible for conducting the audit. WHEN: The audit should be conducted within the timeframe specified in the audit plan. HOW: Use established audit methodologies and tools to assess compliance with EDD requirements. WHERE: The audit should cover all relevant systems, departments, and geographic locations involved in EDD.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for reporting audit findings and implementing follow-up actions to address identified deficiencies.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Prepare a comprehensive audit report detailing findings, recommendations, and action items. WHO: The lead auditor is responsible for drafting the report, while the compliance officer oversees the follow-up actions. WHEN: The audit report must be completed within 15 days of the audit conclusion, and follow-up actions should be initiated immediately thereafter. HOW: Utilize a standardized reporting template to ensure consistency and clarity in findings. WHERE: The report should be distributed to senior management and relevant stakeholders within the organization.
